what_it_does: This file declares the ROUTINE metadata for the dragnet asset staleness check. It defines the routine id, label, description, prompt, working directory, permission mode, daily cadence, trigger, repeats, and max turns. The prompt instructs the routine to list baked extraction assets, compare each asset's bake time and record count against its source corpus, surface a single Gap/Notice to the lead when an asset is stale, and propose a rebake through the dragnet_extract flow without ever running the bake itself.
observations: Declares itself the OWNER of the deep dragnet staleness check
Freshness is treated as a declared computed flag plus a routine, never an automatic rebake
Daily cadence is every:86400 with an additional fire-on-demand trigger
Permission mode is default, described as reads plus a channel post plus a propose-only flow
The prompt maps asset sources: full to claude-sessions, visual-dna to the Visual-DNA vault, and others to inferred sources with an honest no-guess rule
Max turns is 10 and repeats is True
No executable implementation code is present beyond the ROUTINE dictionary and docstring
standouts: Explicitly forbids automatic rebaking and keeps the ~4h bake operator-only via a --confirm door
Surfaces staleness as a single Gap/Notice plus a runnable rebake proposal rather than silently fixing it
Positions itself as the single owner routine for a declared freshness flag used by determine()
conventions: Follows a ROUTINE dictionary schema with id, label, description, prompt, cwd, permission_mode, cadence, repeats, max_turns, and trigger
Uses a docstring to record design rationale, Q6 resolution, and economics
Prompt is written as imperative step-by-step instructions for an agent
Honest no-op behavior is required when all assets are fresh
concerns: Staleness detection relies on heuristic mtime and chunk-count comparisons rather than content hashes
Source inference for unknown asset names is left vague and depends on the agent not guessing
Hard-coded paths appear in the prompt without any file-local validation or abstraction
No error handling, logging, or test harness is defined in this file
notes: A routine descriptor that owns the policy and prompt for detecting stale dragnet extraction assets while keeping expensive rebakes human-gated.
questions: What is the exact ROUTINE registry schema and how is this file loaded at runtime?
How does the dragnet_extract flow accept out_name and scope filters?
What numeric threshold defines 'materially past' or a materially larger source count?
Who is 'the lead' and how is the active channel determined?
What is the Q6 resolution context referenced in the docstring?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches what the file does: it is a routine-owner descriptor that encodes the freshness-check policy and prompt.
apparent_themes: declared freshness flags versus automatic rebakes
intention_signals: It tries to prevent silent use of stale dragnet extraction assets by making staleness detection a dedicated, scheduled, human-gated routine rather than an automatic rebake.
novelty: It explicitly inverts the usual freshness pattern by forbidding automatic rebaking and instead surfacing a proposal, treating a ~4h bake as operator-only.
contribution: Provides the policy, cadence, and prompt specification for the dragnet freshness routine, ensuring staleness is detected and surfaced without automatic expensive rebakes.
summary_for_embedding: A routine descriptor file that defines the dragnet asset freshness check, instructing an agent to compare baked extraction assets against their source corpora and propose a human-gated rebake.
intention_for_embedding: To own the deep staleness check for dragnet extraction assets and surface rebake proposals while keeping the expensive bake operator-only.
